method_atom: A unified framework for driving a weakly damped harmonic mode toward its lowest-energy quantum state by linearly coupling it to a driven auxiliary resonant mode, comparing two generic dissipation-engineering strategies: reservoir/back-action extraction (detuned coupling to the auxiliary mode that biases energy exchange outward) versus measurement-based feedback (continuous readout followed by an applied velocity-proportional damping force). It derives the ultimate fundamental limit of each strategy across the full stable parameter range and shows the crossover is governed by the ratio of the auxiliary mode's bandwidth to the target mode's characteristic frequency — back-action extraction winning when the bandwidth is narrow, feedback damping winning when it is broad.
object_atom: A micromechanical oscillator cooled to its quantum ground state through radiation-pressure coupling with a driven optical cavity, via the experimentally realized back-action (detuned-cavity) cooling and cold-damping quantum-feedback cooling schemes.
brief_reason: The transferable part is the coupled-oscillator/auxiliary-mode cooling machinery and the back-action-vs-feedback limit comparison keyed on a bandwidth-to-frequency ratio; the cavity-optomechanical mechanical resonator with radiation-pressure coupling is the physics-specific object.
